advantages: Merge preserves the true story of when work happened and when it converged.
Rebase produces clean linear history that makes `git log`, `git bisect` and code review easier.
Interactive rebase polishes messy local commits into a coherent narrative before sharing.
Both tools coexist — teams pick policies, not religions.
disadvantages: Rebase rewrites SHAs — dangerous on shared branches.
Merge commits clutter `git log --oneline` and can obscure the order of work.
Long-lived branches accumulate conflict pain regardless of which tool you use.
Misuse (force-push after a bad rebase, merge of an unreviewed branch) can lose teammates' work.
